title: Use Total amount or Total Summary in formula
excerpt: How can i use the total amount of the column into a formula? i mean if the summary column is 100 i want to use that value into a formula. is that possible?

posts.author: SAMlatinoamerica (SAM)
posts.cookedHtml: How can i use the total amount of the column into a formula? i mean if the summary column is 100 i want to use that value into a formula. is that possible?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SAMlatinoamerica,

This is not currently possible in base monday. You would need to use something like Integromat or possibly a marketplace app to provide that.

Jim

posts.author: SAMlatinoamerica (SAM)
posts.cookedHtml: do you know some marketplace app to do this?

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SAMlatinoamerica @JCorrell there is a marketplace app that can do that. The Analytics & Reports by Screenful add-on allows creating charts that combine data from any number of monday boards and has a calculated field that allows calculations between columns.

https://screenful.com/blog/introducing-table-chart-with-custom-formulas-

You can place those charts in monday dashboards

Screenful

Add Screenful charts and reports to Monday dashboard as widgets — Screenful

Start by creating a Monday dashboard if you haven't done that already. Add a widget to this dashboard by clicking the Add a widget button and selecting Apps as shown in the screenshot below.

Notice that in order to make calculation based on the column summaries, you need to group the table chart by “data source (monday board)”. That means that each row in the chart corresponds to the total per column and the calculated field will can use those totals. Reach out to support@screenful.com if you need any assistance.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SAMlatinoamerica,

I took a look at the marketplace. Other than possibly the app that Sami mentioned, I didn’t see anything.

If you are interested, I can show you how to do it with Integromat.

Jim

posts.author: SAMlatinoamerica (SAM)
posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell yes please Jim how can i do that with integromat? o with zapier (i use more zapier than integromat) thank you very much for your help

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@SAMlatinoamerica

I just posted a video that shows the basics on how to do this in Integromat:

I don’t know Zapier nearly as well. I’m sure this could be done with Zapier.

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)
